summary: Small rectangular single-storey building with a flat brick roof inset and a raised stone border; walls combine a lower sand-colored textured block, a middle band of light smooth blocks, and a top brick course with a stone slab coping. Windows are a continuous horizontal row of glass panes around the mid-wall. A low white stone perimeter ledge/step surrounds the building within a shallow trench.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular box with a recessed flat roof and continuous window band on the sides
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical along the long axis (both long sides match); short sides appear symmetric as well

materials.name: quartz
materials.role: middle wall band and corner pillars and low perimeter ledge

elements: continuous horizontal window band (multiple adjacent glass panes at mid-height)
single-storey rectangular wall stack: lower textured block layer, smooth middle band, brick top band
flat recessed roof with stone slab coping
raised white perimeter ledge/step around the building set into surrounding earth
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangle footprint ~11x19 blocks (estimate) and raise walls about 5 blocks high to window band midpoint.

Use sandstone (or sand-colored textured blocks) for the lower 2 layers of walls, a smooth light block (quartz) for the band above, then a 1-block-high brick course near the top. Cap with stone slabs around a recessed flat brick roof.
Place a continuous row of glass panes one block above the lower sandstone section spanning the long sides with regular spacing (in images there are 5 windows across the long face).

Add corner vertical smooth blocks (quartz-like) that frame the middle band on each corner, and a 1-block-high white ledge around the base extending one block out from the wall placed on the ground level.
uncertainties: Exact block types: sandstone and quartz are visually matched but could be other similar light/sand-colored or smooth white blocks.
Footprint exact dimensions: counted from imagery but may be off by ±1–2 blocks; width/depth are estimates.
Height: internal floor/ceiling thickness unknown; only external visible wall height used.
There may be a small entrance or step on one short side not fully visible in all views; entrance location uncertain.
